svg.text: KV Cache Allocation and Prefix Match Flow
alloc_token_slots() full flow: Prefix match → LRU eviction → free page allocation

g.text: STEP 1 · Prefix Match
tree_cache.match_prefix(key: RadixKey | list[int])
mem_cache/radix_cache.py
• Find longest matching Prefix for key in the Radix Tree
• Returns MatchResult(prefix_len, kv_indices)
• On match, reuse cached KV Cache and skip recomputation
Radix Tree matches token-by-token from root, stops at branch point. Time complexity O(L)
STEP 2 · Compute Required Slots
num_new_tokens = num_tokens - prefix_len
mem_cache/common.py:alloc_token_slots()
• num_tokens: total tokens needed for this Forward
• prefix_len: length of the matched Prefix
• num_new_tokens: number of new Token Slots to allocate
STEP 3 · Call evict_from_tree_cache
evict_from_tree_cache(tree_cache, num_tokens, dp_rank=0)
mem_cache/common.py
• Always called; the function checks internally whether available space suffices
• If enough space, returns directly; otherwise triggers LRU eviction
Paged Allocator: allocates by Page (default page_size=1, set by server_args)
STEP 4 · LRU Eviction
tree_cache.evict(num_tokens, dp_rank=None)
mem_cache/radix_cache.py
• Evict from leaf nodes of the Radix Tree in LRU order
• Only leaf nodes are evicted (shared Prefixes protected)
• Release KV Cache indices held by evicted nodes
• Internally calls allocator.free(indices) to return slots to free list
evictable_size = allocator.available_size() + size of evictable Cache
STEP 5 · Allocate New Slots
new_kv_indices = allocator.alloc(num_new_tokens)
mem_cache/allocator.py
• Allocate num_new_tokens slots from the Allocator's free list
• Paged Allocator: allocates whole pages, returns Page IDs
• Token Allocator: allocates individual Token Slots
If allocation fails (still not enough), returns None and triggers Retract or Abort
STEP 6 · Caller Combines Indices
Combination happens at the schedule_batch caller
mem_cache/common.py only returns new_kv_indices
• Callers like ScheduleBatch.prepare_for_extend() concatenate Prefix indices with new ones
• Build out_cache_loc (write positions) and cache_loc (read positions)
• Write to req_to_token_pool for use during model Forward
out_cache_loc points to newly allocated Slots; cache_loc covers Prefix + new Slots
STEP 7 · Return Success
return True
KV Cache allocation succeeded
Request can join the Batch and run model Forward
Key Design Notes
Radix Tree Advantages
• Longest prefix match: O(L) time, matches token-by-token from root
• Structural sharing: shared Prefix stored once, node count = O(branch points)
• LRU eviction protects shared Prefix: only leaves evicted, internal nodes with any child stay
Four-Layer Architecture
• Memory Pool: device-side JAX Array storing actual KV data
• Allocator: maintains free Token/Page list, performs alloc/free
• Prefix Cache: Radix Tree, manages Prefix sharing and LRU eviction
• Coordination: alloc_token_slots() orchestrates the three layers
Paged vs Token Allocator
• Paged: allocates by Page (page_size from server_args, default 1), reduces fragmentation, suits long sequences
• Token: allocates per single Token, flexible but may fragment
Failure Handling
• On allocation failure, Scheduler triggers Retract (releases newest request in running_batch)
• If still insufficient after Retract, Abort some requests
• Ensures the system keeps running under memory pressure
